paragraphs.body: You are watching this painter sitting in a vibrant green, crisp, tree ridden plot of grass, hammocks hanging from tree to tree, the sound of the wind through the trees and leaves all around you, while the sun is shining after the clouds covered it for most of the day. As you take a closer look, you see this beautiful, canvas with paint caked on it, a forgotten paint brush to its right. The paint brush has this right red handle where the paint it is chipping off, the type that gets stuck under your nails and clings to your skin. The bristles are fraying slightly, sticking out straight and to the sides, coated in this bright blue paint. The paint glistens in the sun, looking as if it contains sparkles, the reflecting from the sun. And all you can do is just sit there and admire the scene, not thinking of anything else.

annotations.comment: This is a lovely descriptive paragraph! I wonder, though, why it's different than the original. Selfishly, I miss my favorite line :)

paragraphs.body: Watching someone create art that we admire. Something that brings people together, either shared talent or relishing in the beauty of the painting. A dirty paintbrush helps demonstrate that there is beauty in chaos, how not everything that is “dirty,” not put together, is bad. That good things are able come out of stressful, messy, chaotic situations, how you sometimes have to get a little messy to make something beautiful. Even though you may feel glued, stuck in place, too scared to try and start over, too scared to see if you can make something amazing, something admirable out of the situation. How sometimes you relate to a used, dirty paintbrush, overwhelmed and trapped, just having to remember you may be in the process of something with a beautiful outcome. It is truly something to admire, a word borrowed from Middle French admirer, means to feel respect and approval for (someone or something): to regard with admiration (“Admire,” Dictionary). This word’s meaning has truly shifted over the years it has been utilized. The original, French, meaning meant to “regard with wonder, marvel at.” While being used more frequently over a larger group of people, it slowly shifted to a more Latin meaning of “to regard with pleasure and esteem” (Harper). It really used to carry this heaviness, something that, when used, had a true indescribable meaning. Has now shifted to a word that has a less deep and almost lighter meaning behind it, while we still use it to show great praise of someone, it is more common to be used than it once was.

annotations.text: dirty paintbrush helps demonstrate that there is beauty in chaos, how not everything that is “dirty,” not put together, is bad. That good things are able come out of stressful, messy, chaotic situations, how you sometimes have to get a little messy to make something beautiful.

paragraphs.body: Now, close your eyes and think of someone you truly just admire, no matter the reason or significance think of that person and what it is that makes you admire them. For me, I immediately think of my mother and my two best friends. People I love with my whole heart and revel in their love and determination. I admire my mom because she, as a single mother, has sacrificed everything for me, her free time, her social life, everything, all to ensure that I had everything I could dream of, and we could live a happy life. I can honestly, strangely enough, related some of our situations to the paintbrush. Sometimes it feels suffocating, like paint coating the brush, feeling stuck like there is no way to solve some issues. Her and I have gone through a lot of really hard situations, that had no visible outcome, while in the midst of it. At last, we persevered and made it out, creating beautiful memories together, each learning something from that situation, somehow always ending up okay. Admiring how we stuck with each other during trying times, just like the final painting created with the paintbrush, finally being able to clean the paint off the brushes, regaining the ability to breathe again. But I also admire her for how fun she is, how loving, kind, courageous she is. Her beautiful spirit that lights up every room, her dedication to her job, how she maintains a positive attitude even when the world keeps throwing punches at her, her dedication to me, her forgiveness, attitude, jokes, everything that makes her herself. You do not have to admire someone for how they handle situations, how they have survived things and lived to tell the tail, how they have gone through things that are unimaginable to you. You can also admire someone for just being themselves, being happy, being kind to everyone they meet. You can admire someone you have never met, your coach, your parents, best friends, favorite author, truly anyone who exists, that is the beauty of it.

annotations.text: Sometimes it feels suffocating, like paint coating the brush, feeling stuck like there is no way to solve some issues. Her and I have gone through a lot of really hard situations, that had no visible outcome, while in the midst of it. At last, we persevered and made it out, creating beautiful memories together, each learning something from that situation, somehow always ending up okay.

paragraphs.body: I can relate my best friends to a used paintbrush as well, how they always make every situation beautiful, how they can bring out the best in anyone, how they leave their mark on everyone they have ever met. A used paintbrush will leave marks on every surface, every person, every canvas, leading to artwork created in the most unexpected places. Like a paintbrush, nothing in my two best friends path goes untouched, unpainted with their gorgeous, admirable, perfect, personalities. Seeing how the sunshine they radiate, that is engrained in them, has effects on everyone around them, strangers, acquaintances, parents, anyone truly. Being able to witness them facing their college careers head on, starting the next chapter of their life, one I get the privilege of living with them. I can say with full sincerity, pride, and love that they have left their mark on me permanently, creating me to be the best person I can be just by simply being in their presences and having the gift and privilege of maintaining a very close friendship with them.

annotations.comment: There are admirable qualities and traits that your friends and your mother seem to share. What are those traits, specifically? Let's talk :)
annotations.context_left: every canvas, leading to artwork created in the most unexpected places. Like a paintbrush,

paragraphs.body: Sometimes situations, life, people, places turn out to be like a dirty used paintbrush. Where you feel stuck in place, like you are not able to move anywhere, to grow, to breathe. But you have to remember that these situations will have a beautiful outcome, even if it is not what you originally expected to happen, even if you have to convince yourself that it is okay. Everything happens for a reason. How you handle said situations, head on, strong, resilient, all create an extremely admirable person. Even if you do not find yourself lucky enough to not be facing these situations, try to have a positive beautiful spirit that uplifts everyone around you. Make your mark. Try your best every day to leave hints of your spirit everywhere, do not leave a person, place, surface, idea, untouched by you. Be like a used paintbrush, that makes something beautiful. That has the ability to be cleaned off and used again in creation. That can brighten someone’s day up just being with it for a few hours, that can work with others, that has the durability to withstand hard things, that leaves their mark. That all in all, is able to grow, change, help, create, love, all without losing its original self, even if the bristles change a little over time. Works Cited “Admire.” Merriam-Webster.com Dictionary, Merriam-Webster, https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/admire Harper, Douglas. “Etymology of admire.” Online Etymology Dictionary, https://www.etymonline.come/word/admire
